
SPI-M-O and the 2009 H1N1 Pandemic

hine report: https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/independent-review-into-the-response-to-the-2009-swine-flu-pandemic

DH: https://assets.publishing.service.gov.uk/media/5a79d52240f0b670a8025c17/doh.pdf

HPA: chrome-extension://efaidnbmnnnibpcajpcglclefindmkaj/https://assets.publishing.service.gov.uk/media/5a79ec1140f0b670a80266c6/hpa-march31_0.pdf

GOS: chrome-extension://efaidnbmnnnibpcajpcglclefindmkaj/https://assets.publishing.service.gov.uk/media/5a79cf15ed915d042206b304/govt-office-science_0.pdf

---

info: https://webarchive.nationalarchives.gov.uk/ukgwa/+/www.dh.gov.uk/ab/SPI/DH_095904

consensus statement: https://webarchive.nationalarchives.gov.uk/ukgwa/20110609133736/http://www.dh.gov.uk/ab/SPI/DH_118862

archive of: https://webarchive.nationalarchives.gov.uk/ukgwa/20110609133736/http://www.dh.gov.uk/ab/SPI/DH_118862

---

Epidemiological questions:

- interpreting emerging unknown epi parameters
- informing novel data collection for surveillance
- preparing for and detecting a second wave
- ? immune response

Process questions:

- elicitation process, handling conflict & expressing dissent, avoiding groupthink, role as quality assurance (see GOS & HPA)
- function of modelling from SPI-MO vs central government / ALBs (see HPA); capacity demands, incentives and compensation
- clarifying policy objectives, communicating evidence and advice, role of secretariat
